files.html:
On The Gravitational Nature Of Time

A sample note & a novel interpretation of relativity yielding multiple directly observed quantities to well within measurement error.

Thank you


Thank you for taking the time to read this. My story is posted elsewhere, so to keep things short, this is the entire reason I built Fluster & Conundrum in the first place.

Almost 5 years ago I realized Einstein made an assumption that made far more sense before the observations that give us the Big Bang, and after playing around with the math I realized I can derive an absolutely staggering amount of physics from a single, mathematically equivalent modification to Einstein's model. I quit my job, became homeless, and over the course of this pursuit built the original version of Fluster for my own personal use.

I'll do my best to convince the math types among you (this article does assume some basic understanding of physics and relativity) by the end of this sample note that Einstein made a mistake1, but regardless of your opinions on the model, I hope you enjoy Fluster and find it as useful as I have. Thanks for checking it out!

Also, please keep in mind that while this may not be as responsive as the Fluster website, it was written in the very early stages of a markdown like syntax with the same transpiler that powers Fluster. The Conundrum transpiler will continue to improve daily, with blogging support being a major priority.

 percent_diff.py
 
def percentDiff(observed, derived):
    return (observed - derived) / observed * 100

Also, keep in mind that if we wanted to be selective we can choose a radius here on Earth's surface that wll satisfy the above equations perfectly, giving an error of 0. The radii that were used were whichever was most fitting for the job... the mean radius for calculating volume, the polar radius when spin was involved to minimize centripital influence, and Astropy provided values (equatorial) for everything else.

The Lighthouse & The Clocktower

An opening thought experiment

Consider two observers, AAA and BBB. Let AAA and BBB agree that BBB should travel between two arbitrary points in space2, p1p_1p1​ and p2p_2p2​ at an agreed upon velocity vvv.

At p2p_2p2​ place a time keeping device that remains visible from p1p_1p1​. Allow that AAA remains at rest relative to p1p_1p1​, and very near p1p_1p1​ while BBB goes in motion (at an inertial pace) between p1p_1p1​ and p2p_2p2​.

At what time does AAA observe BBB reach p2p_2p2​? If the value is not d/vd/vd/v, but γd/v\gamma d/vγd/v, then v≠v′v \neq v_\primev=v′​, unless d→dγd \to d\gammad→dγ. The only possible solution that allows for Einstein's additional factor of γ\gammaγ while allowing the observer in an unchanged state to observe the kinetic effects of observer in motion is that the distance between p1p_{1}p1​ and p2p_{2}p2​ must elongate by a factor of γ \gammaγ.

Is this not inline with what we already believe? Do current models not describe cosmic inflation as a time dependent property? Does more time not pass for the observer in relative motion? Of course p1p_{1}p1​ and p2p_{2}p2​ will be further seperated, if everything dilates, not just the space in-between galaxies. If we presume that this dilation Einstein applied to time may applied to space in the coordinate system of the observer in relative motion, than we can describe this spatial dilation as the mechanism to the equivalence principle and combine cosmic inflation, gravitational acceleration and time itself into a single process as observed from different reference frames!

For the physics professionals: Yes, this model violates the Lorentz transformations, but it does so while satisfying every single experimental validation of either SR or GR. Don't stop reading yet, it's just about to get good.

Time Gravity Equivalence

Let's recognize the two primary properties that time occupies:

  • A 4th displacement coordinate
  • A driving rate of change

By attributing the 4th displacement coordinate to the density axis that arises from this geometry and the driving rate of change to motion, not time, we can remove our temporal axis entirely! Time becomes nothing more than a mathematical tool... a ratio of other changing properties, all in response to the primary rate of change: motion.

Of course we need to change the dimensionality of much of our physics, but as I will demonstrate here... the math remains remarkably consistent.

Does this not make more sense? A Universe driven by motion as a driving rate of change instead of an abstract quantity that we've never directly measured, time?

Our Cosmological Velocity

Let's consider that this dilation of space might give rise to our gravitational acceleration, where p1p_1p1​ becomes akin to the cosmological rest frame. If we allow that this velocity gives rise to spatial dilation, which then gives rise to the gravitational acceleration that we feel, we should examine the derivative of ggg with respect to RRR.

δ=ddR−GMR2=2GMR3 \delta = \frac{d}{dR} - G \frac{M}{R^2} = 2 G \frac{M}{R^3} δ=dRd​−GR2M​=2GR3M​
1
Hint The negative sign here is applied to ggg since it is space that is moving, not the observer. This gives the observe a negative net x˙\dot{x}x˙ despite the fact that ΔEkinetic=0\Delta E_{\text{kinetic}} = 0ΔEkinetic​=0.

If we take the average of this integral, we find a scalar product that can be used in the same manner as γ\gammaγ:

1R∫0R02GMR3=1+gR \frac{1}{R} \int_0^{R_0} 2 G \frac{M}{R^3} = 1 + \frac{g}{R} R1​∫0R0​​2GR3M​=1+Rg​
2

If we solve γ\gammaγ for vvv, we find:

vγ=c1−1γ2 v_\gamma = c \sqrt{1 - \frac{1}{\gamma^2}} vγ​=c1−γ21​​
3

Substituting the value from equation 2 for γ\gammaγ we find:

vγ=c1−1(1+gR)2=526.6 km s−1 v_\gamma = c \sqrt{1 - \frac{1}{\left(1 + \frac{g}{R}\right)^2}} = 526.6~ \text{km}~ \text{s}^{-1} vγ​=c1−(1+Rg​)21​​=526.6 km s−1
4

This value coincides closely with direct observation, but due to the wide error margins inherent in these types of observations, we're going to have to do better. Don't worry... we can.

Our Kinematic Velocity

Let's consider the fact that Earth kind of has two velocities, our kinematic velocity (the motion through space), and our cosmological velocity (the motion through space plus the motion of space). Since we just derived our cosmological velocity, we should now remove the temporal axis, giving us a velocity within a contained 3-dimensional 'slice'. This gives a velocity relative to the 'dough' in the common raisin bread analogy of cosmic inflation.

Let's first recognize a time dependent quantity as a dimensionless ratio of distances:

dx+dsdx∝Δt \frac{dx + ds}{dx} \propto \Delta t dxdx+ds​∝Δt
5

If we consider that time has no driving properties anymore, we should then turn our attention to motion. Since this model proposes that dsdsds (cosmic inflation and now gravitational acceleration) is time, we can find this time dependent quantity as follows:

Δτ=∫px˙∫0vx˙ \Delta \tau = \frac{\int_p \dot{x}}{\int_0^v \dot{x}} Δτ=∫0v​x˙∫p​x˙​
6

Notation


This notation is likely confusing, so let me clarify. To satisfy the properties time occupies, this quantity is integrated in a manner that is unique, keeping the denominator fixed at 1 unit of distance (the velocity in units of distance) while the integral ∫p\int_p∫p​ is meant to indicate a 'proportional' integral, or an integral that goes on indefinitely as 'time' progresses.

This gives us a dimensionless quantity that is distance/distance, of a magnitude that is equivalent to ttt.

Let's then re-examine equation 4 and it's derivation. Since dxdxdx in equation 4 is per unit time, we should then divide by vγv_\gammavγ​ and multiply by our time equivalent quantity from equation 6 .

This gives:

∫0R∫0x[−2GM⊕R⊕31vγτ] dR dτ \int_0^R \int_0^x \left[ -2 G \frac{M_\oplus}{R_\oplus^3} \frac{1}{v_\gamma} \tau \right]~ dR ~ d\tau ∫0R​∫0x​[−2GR⊕3​M⊕​​vγ​1​τ] dR dτ
7

And following the rules of integration defined above, we find:

∫0R∫0x[δ1vγτ] dR dτ=1+12GM⊕R⊕3x˙x^ \int_0^R \int_0^x \left[ \delta \frac{1}{v_\gamma} \tau \right]~ dR ~ d\tau = 1 + \frac{1}{2} \frac{G M_\oplus}{R^3_\oplus} \frac{\dot{x}}{\hat{x}} ∫0R​∫0x​[δvγ​1​τ] dR dτ=1+21​R⊕3​GM⊕​​x^x˙​
8
Notation Here and throughout the rest of this note, x˙\dot{x}x˙ does not indicate a derivative with respect to time, but rather a fundamental, driving rate of change. x^\hat{x}x^ is a notation used to demonstrate the magnitude equivalent of 111 for that velocity, making x^=v\hat{x} = vx^=v in units of distance. I'm hesitant to say x^=v 1t\hat{x} = v~1tx^=v 1t, because while mathematically acccurate, I feel that it hides underlying physics, or rather invents a derivative that is not an inherent part of the system only to remove it by multiplying by ttt.

If we substitute this result back into equation 4 we find:

vγ′=c1−1(1+12GMR3)2=371,567 km s−1 v_\gamma^\prime = c \sqrt{1 - \frac{1}{\left( 1 + \frac{1}{2} G \frac{M}{R^3} \right)^2}} = 371,567 ~ \text{km} ~ \text{s}^{-1} vγ′​=c1−(1+21​GR3M​)21​​=371,567 km s−1
9

Observational ranges for this value that we can observe much more reliably is between 368,145−369,943 km s−1368,145-369,943~ \text{km}~ \text{s}^{-1}368,145−369,943 km s−1, giving us a margin of error of:

Error Margin: 0.43%−0.92%0.43\%-0.92\%0.43%−0.92%
From Orbital Parameters

Let's validate this model by testing this relationship one more time.

Consider the above diagram. If this model is correct, we can improve the derivation of our orbital behavior. Instead of the following:

Fcentripital=Fgravity F_{\text{centripital}} = F_{\text{gravity}} Fcentripital​=Fgravity​
10

We can solve this trigonometrically.

R02+x˙2dt2=(∫0R0−2GMR3)2=(R0+g)2 R_0^2 + \dot{x}^2 dt^2 = \left(\int_0^{R_0} -2 G \frac{M}{R^3}\right)^2 = \left(R_0 + g\right)^2 R02​+x˙2dt2=(∫0R0​​−2GR3M​)2=(R0​+g)2
11

Solving this equality for dtdtdt gives only one non-zero solution:

dt=−2GMR3G2M2−R4x˙2 dt = - \frac{2 G M R^{3}}{G^{2} M^{2} - R^{4} \dot{x}^{2}} dt=−G2M2−R4x˙22GMR3​
12

If we substitute in x˙\dot{x}x˙ for it's Newtonian value, we find:

dt=R03(112GM−R03)=1.00000077 dt = R_0^3 \left(\frac{1}{\frac{1}{2} GM - R_0^3}\right) = 1.00000077 dt=R03​(21​GM−R03​1​)=1.00000077
13

As relativity predicts this value does not give 1, but a magnitude slightly higher as γ>1\gamma \gt 1γ>1.

Pluging this value in to the equation we used to find δ\deltaδ gives:

v=c1−1dt2=373,453 km s−1 v = c \sqrt{1 - \frac{1}{dt^2}} = 373,453 ~ \text{km} ~ \text{s}^{-1} v=c1−dt21​​=373,453 km s−1
14

This value again coincides closely with our velocity observed relative to the CMB dipole, once again laying credibility to the model we are proposing... gravitational acceleration is a velocity dependent property.

In the relativistic frame

As we used the Newtonian derived orbital parameters in the previous section to derive a relativistic velocity correction in the form of an increase in elapsed 'time', we can now derive a relativistic equation for a body in stable orbit by setting dt=1dt = 1dt=1 and solving for x˙\dot{x}x˙. This gives a model in the coordinate system of the observer in motion, as he travels at the 'agreed upon velocity' (any velocity multiplied by a scalar of 1).

If we extend our lighthouse & clocktower example to an orbital frame of reference, where BBB orbits AAA at an agreed upon velocity vov_ovo​, then this velocity would be decreased by a factor of dtdtdt in the coordinate system of AAA due to the decreased spatial density of SB\mathbf{S}_BSB​, while our relativistically corrected orbital velocity equation becomes as follows:

2GMR→GM(GM+2R3)R2 \sqrt{\frac{2 GM}{R}} \quad \to \quad \frac{\sqrt{G M \left(G M + 2 R^{3}\right)}}{R^{2}} R2GM​​→R2GM(GM+2R3)​​
15

When we solve for RRR and plugin our galactic parameters here on Earth, we find a radius of 8.13 Kpc8.13~ Kpc8.13 Kpc, perfectly on top of observatonal values, without any additional unseen mass!

Electromagnetism

Consider the determinant of a 3-dimensional, diagonal matrix, representing the change in volume of the coordinate system.

s=∣ω000ω000ω ∣=ω3 s = \left|\begin{matrix} \omega & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & \omega & 0 \\ 0 & 0 &\omega\ \end{matrix}\right| = \omega^3 s=​ω00​0ω0​00ω ​​=ω3
16

Not only does this accomodate existing experimental validations of SR & GR, it does so while accomodating the original relativistic experiment: Michelson & Morely. The only direction they didn't evaluate was along the radial vector, precisely the direction described by this determinant.

If we consider that the magnitude of δ\deltaδ is this determinant, we should then take it's cube root:

ω=δ3=2GM⊕R⊕33=0.01453 1s \omega = \sqrt[3]{\delta} = \sqrt[3]{\frac{2 G M_\oplus}{R_\oplus^3}} = 0.01453 ~ \frac{1}{\text{s}} ω=3δ​=3R⊕3​2GM⊕​​​=0.01453 s1​
17

Woah... that number looks familiar that's 12α±0.39%\frac{1}{2} \alpha \pm 0.39\%21​α±0.39%! As a matter of fact, when you solve for the 2 , the solution is actually:

2−α±0.025% 2 - \alpha \pm 0.025\% 2−α±0.025%
18

Giving a final equation of:

α=12−α2GM⊕R⊕33±0.025% \boxed{\alpha = \frac{1}{2 - \alpha} \sqrt[3]{2 \frac{G M_\oplus}{R_\oplus^3}} \pm 0.025\%} α=2−α1​32R⊕3​GM⊕​​​±0.025%​
19

Solving this equation for α\alphaα gives:

α=1±1−(2GM⊕R⊕3)1/3=1.9927 or 0.0072953 \alpha = 1 \pm \sqrt{1 - \left(\frac{2 G M_\oplus}{R_\oplus^3}\right)^{1/3}} = 1.9927 \text{ or } 0.0072953 α=1±1−(R⊕3​2GM⊕​​)1/3​=1.9927 or 0.0072953
20

That's almost precisely the value we were looking for when we add these values, and almost exactly α\alphaα again when we subtract them, giving very Fibonnaci-like vibes.

Final Error: 0.025%0.025\%0.025%
Take a second to recogize the fact that in the exact function we predicted to find a unifying quantity, the single most unifying electromagnetic property arises. This is not a dimensional coincidence.

Using our solar parameters, equivalent to spinor=(12)1/3\text{spinor}=\left( \frac{1}{2} \right)^{1/3}spinor=(21​)1/3

Let's consider the fact that we have a set of unique properties for two known bodies, us, and the sun. First, we know that all of our observational 'constants' are accurate here. Second, we know that the Sun is the dominant gravitational object in the vicinity. Let's take advantage of this fact by presuming that the lower limit of this 'spinor' approximation is found at the Sun.

Let's consider this function where the spinor=(12)1/3\text{spinor}=\left( \frac{1}{2} \right)^{1/3}spinor=(21​)1/3, or the same quantity before the torsion applied by α\alphaα and the dilation applied by dsdsds.

Plugging this back into equation 19 gives:

(12−α)1/32GM⊕R⊕33=GM⊙R⊙33=α±0.0034% \left(\frac{1}{2 - \alpha}\right)^{1/3} \sqrt[3]{2 \frac{G M_\oplus}{R_\oplus^3}} = \sqrt[3]{\frac{GM_\odot}{R_\odot^3}} = \alpha \pm 0.0034\% (2−α1​)1/332R⊕3​GM⊕​​​=3R⊙3​GM⊙​​​=α±0.0034%
21

Demonstrating that this function is more than just a coincidence.

Error 1±α1 \pm \alpha1±α with an error of 0.00347%0.00347\%0.00347% and 0.003427%0.003427\%0.003427% respectively.
Significance of α\alphaα

If you're unfamiliar with physics, let me take a second to break down how profound this is. That's an electromagnetic constant, comprised of multiple electromagnetic constants defined in terms of gravitational properties in what certainly looks like a recursive function.

This is what α\alphaα looks like completely written out:

α=14πe2ℏμ0ϵ0 \alpha = \frac{1}{4 \pi}\frac{e^2}{\hbar}\sqrt{\frac{\mu_0}{\epsilon_0}} α=4π1​ℏe2​ϵ0​μ0​​​
22
Component What it does
1/4π1/4\pi1/4π A consequence of the spherical geometry
ϵ0\epsilon_0ϵ0​ The electric constant
μ0\mu_0μ0​ The magnetic constant
eee The fundamental charge
ℏ\hbarℏ The momentum carrying quantity of a massless photon.
1μ0ϵ0\frac{1}{\sqrt{\mu_0 \epsilon_0}}μ0​ϵ0​​1​ The speed of light, and therefore the speed of causality.

That's basically the relationship of all of electromagnetism described in one function. Don't worry though, we can keep going! Now that we've derived a relationship to α \alphaα, we can take advantage of the electromagnetic equations. Let's take advantage of the fact that the electromagnetic tensor is undefined at Δt=0\Delta t = 0Δt=0. If electromagnetism can not exist without time, maybe time is masking a subset of electromagnetic properties.

Electric Field Divergence Equivalent

Let's start by assuming the following identity:

∇⋅E=c(∇⋅B) \nabla \cdot E = c \left(\nabla \cdot B\right) ∇⋅E=c(∇⋅B)
23

Since

∇⋅S=δ \nabla \cdot \mathbf{S} = \delta ∇⋅S=δ
24

then

∇⋅B=δ \nabla \cdot \mathbf{B} = \delta ∇⋅B=δ
25

and

∇⋅E=cδ=921.116 meters \nabla \cdot \mathbf{E} = c \delta = 921.116 ~\text{meters} ∇⋅E=cδ=921.116 meters
26

If we then multiply equation 26 by our spinor approximation, converting this linear spatial dilation to rotation, we find:

12−αcδ=462.244 meters \frac{1}{2 - \alpha} c \delta = 462.244~ \text{meters} 2−α1​cδ=462.244 meters
27

That's our rotational velocity on Earth's equator ±−0.006778%\pm -0.006778\%±−0.006778%! Off by a total of 0.03 meters on a radius the size of Earth's!

Dimensionality


As in traditional models,

x˙=v=[dxdt] \dot{x} = v = \left[\frac{dx}{dt}\right] x˙=v=[dtdx​]
28

This model uses a velocity that is

x˙=v=[dxds] \dot{x} = v = \left[\frac{dx}{ds}\right] x˙=v=[dsdx​]
29

When multiplied by our 'time' equivalent ds=δds = \deltads=δ, this gives

x˙ ds=distance \dot{x}~ ds = \text{distance} x˙ ds=distance
30
In the same manner that vt=distancevt = \text{distance}vt=distance

Velocity as a function of α\alphaα

Let's consider equation 19 once again. If we re-arrange this equation such that dsdsds is isolatd on one side, we find:

α(2−α)=ds=δ1/3 \alpha \left( 2 - \alpha \right)= ds = \delta^{1/3} α(2−α)=ds=δ1/3
31

Cubing δ\deltaδ to bring the units of time back to a power of 1 gives:

(α(2−α))3=ds3=δ \left(\alpha \left( 2 - \alpha \right) \right)^3 = ds^3 = \delta (α(2−α))3=ds3=δ
32

If we consider that all velocities are proportional in magnitude to ccc, we should not look for vvv, but for β2\beta^2β2. This then gives:

(α(2−α))3=ds3dx′=δc⇒v=c(α(2−α))3=525,693 meters \left(\alpha \left( 2 - \alpha \right) \right)^3 = \frac{ds^3}{dx^{\prime}} = \frac{\delta}{c} \quad \Rightarrow \quad v = c \sqrt{\left(\alpha \left( 2 - \alpha \right) \right)^3} = 525,693 ~ \text{meters} (α(2−α))3=dx′ds3​=cδ​⇒v=c(α(2−α))3​=525,693 meters
33

This is our original velocity ±0.0415%\pm 0.0415\%±0.0415%. One can very logically make an argument for the small-angle approximations being a cause for coincidence here, but as a function of α\alphaα?

Then, consider the electromagnetic displacement current J\mathbf{{J}}J where

J=ρev0 \mathbf{{J}} = \rho_e v_0 J=ρe​v0​

∇⋅E=ρeϵ0 \nabla \cdot E = \frac{{\rho_e}}{{\epsilon_0}} ∇⋅E=ϵ0​ρe​​

37
Note: There is a radius here on Earth's surface where this value is precisely Φ\PhiΦ, the Golden Ratio.

Earth's Orbital Velocity From α\alphaα

Consider the radius-independent magnitude of our metric for the dominant body in our system, the Sun:

vg=GM⊙3 v_{g} = \sqrt[3]{GM_\odot} vg​=3GM⊙​​

Considering that nothing in this model is proportional to time, we should consider this model as proportional to dxdxdx, or orbital velocity.

vgvorbit=171.25209 \frac{v_{g}}{v_{\text{orbit}}} = 171.25209 vorbit​vg​​=171.25209

vgvorbit=ηAUR \frac{v_{g}}{v_{\text{orbit}}} = \eta \frac{\mathbf{AU}}{R} vorbit​vg​​=ηRAU​

η=GM⊙32πAU2TyearR⊕=α±0.05443% \eta = \frac{\sqrt[3]{GM_\odot}}{2 \pi \mathbf{AU}^2} T_{\text{year}} R_{\oplus} = \alpha \pm 0.05443\% η=2πAU23GM⊙​​​Tyear​R⊕​=α±0.05443%

Mass Density Approximation

Finally, let's wrap this all up with one last approximation. Consider the following, that we've derived above:

β02=1−1(1+gR)2≈(α(2−α))3 \beta_0^2 = 1 - \frac{1}{ \left(1 + \frac{g}{R}\right)^2 } \approx \left( \alpha \left( 2 - \alpha \right) \right)^3 β02​=1−(1+Rg​)21​≈(α(2−α))3
42

Then considering that

ρ=MV=M43πR3=3M4πR3 \rho = \frac{M}{V} = \frac{M}{\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3} = \frac{3M}{4\pi R^3} ρ=VM​=34​πR3M​=4πR33M​
43

and the fact that we defined

δ=2GM⊕R⊕3 \delta = 2 \frac{G M_\oplus}{R_{\oplus}^3} δ=2R⊕3​GM⊕​​
44

Then

MR3=δ2G \frac{M}{R^3} = \frac{\delta}{2G} R3M​=2Gδ​
45

and

ρ=34π(δ2G)=3δ8πG \rho = \frac{3}{4\pi} \left(\frac{\delta}{2G}\right) = \frac{3 \delta}{8 \pi G} ρ=4π3​(2Gδ​)=8πG3δ​
46

And given the relationship that

δ≈Φv0 \delta \approx \frac{\Phi}{v_0} δ≈v0​Φ​
47

and

β2≈(α(2−α))3 \beta^2 \approx \left(\alpha \left(2 - \alpha\right)\right)^3 β2≈(α(2−α))3
48

then finally

ρ=3(δR32G)4πR3=3δ8πG=ϕGcα(2−α)=5561.006gm3 \rho = \frac{3 \left( \frac{\delta R^{3}}{2G} \right)}{4 \pi R^{3}} = \frac{3 \delta}{8 \pi G} = \frac{\phi}{G c \alpha \left(2 - \alpha\right)} = 5561.006 \frac{\text{g}}{m^3} ρ=4πR33(2GδR3​)​=8πG3δ​=Gcα(2−α)ϕ​=5561.006m3g​
49

This is our observed mass density, m0m_{0}m0​...

ρ=M⊕43πR3±0.85% \rho = \frac{M_{\oplus}}{\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3} \pm 0.85\% ρ=34​πR3M⊕​​±0.85%
50

That's an error of under 1% for an approximation, that's using approximations and transcental quantities, demonstrating the wide application of this model.

Supportive Arguments

  • No terminating geodesics, and no more blackholes. Singularities become nothing but a mirage in the distance that vanishes as you approach that gravitational field.
  • The Bullet Cluster observations are heavily indicative of a repulsive gravity model.
  • This notion of time paints an entirely different picture of chronology, where there is no limitation on backwards time travel, but a limitation on affecting one's own past. Go back to t−nt - nt−n and try to kill your grandfather. He won't be there anymore as he's now at ttt, if he's still around. Don't get me started on the potentials around massless conciousness in this buoyant, 4-dimensional environment.
  • Machian inertia. This model concludes that the Universe is almost precisely as Mach had predicted, with inertia being comprised of the sum of this radiating force for all other bodies in the Universe.
  • The entire equivalence principle. We've all become used to it so we overlook just how profound it is, but it's literally assuming what I'm saying is true mathematically and then just pretending it's not in reality.
  • The lack of a magnetic monopole, or rather, the existince of an electric monopole. Where do you think that 'sink and faucet' model is funelling charge to? Charge is just space flowing through a hole in an equi-temporal plane, while charged bodies are just bodies with a higher tendency towards one side of this plane due to the buoyant forces along this density axis.
  • The Fermi paradox. There's an entirely other dimension that life might exist across that we have been so far unable to transcend.
  • Relative motion. Think about it. Do we currently calculate all motion as being truly relative, or do we not already calculate motion as relative to the underlying gravitational acceleration and then lie to undergrads about it? The tensors of GR mask this fact to an extent, but reduce them to their calculus equivalents and we are doing precisely this.

After all of this talk of divergence, it's important to understand the relationship between this geometry, the proposed spatial dilation, and the power 3 in our 3 dimensional coordinate system. If we recall in slight reworking of equation 19 :

Rα=Γ2GM⊕3 R \alpha = \Gamma \sqrt[3]{2GM_\oplus} Rα=Γ32GM⊕​​
51

Where

Γ=12−α \Gamma = \frac{1}{2 - \alpha} Γ=2−α1​
52

Is it a coincidence that Rα3R \alpha^3Rα3 gives the unit of measure, the cubit used by our most ancient civilizations? I'll leave that for you to ponder... With ongoing world events, maybe we'll soon know.

Footnotes

1.
The logical question here is well, why not just publish it in peer review? The only possible mathematical solution indicates that the Earth is expanding with the Universe. Einstein himself couldn't get a model like this past our current peer review culture.
2.
Assuming a uniform gravitational field across the path of travel.

front_matter.title: On the gravitational nature of time
front_matter.summary: A sample note & a novel interpretation of relativity yielding multiple directly observed quantities to well within measurement error
